name: Cycle — Drug Development Lifecycle (7 stages)
input: Show the drug development lifecycle as a circular process: Target Discovery, Lead Identification, Preclinical Testing, Phase I (Safety), Phase II (Efficacy), Phase III (Confirmation), FDA Review & Approval. After approval, post-market surveillance feeds back to Target Discovery for next-generation compounds.
qualityCriteria: Items present (Target Discovery, Lead Identification, Preclinical, Phase I, Phase II, Phase III, FDA Review)
Description for each item is present
No placeholder or lorem ipsum content
napkinBenchmark: Circular diagram with 7 nodes, each labeled with a phase name. Arrows between nodes form a closed loop.

data.title: Drug Development Lifecycle

items.label: Target Discovery
items.description: Identify molecular targets involved in disease pathology

items.label: Lead Identification
items.description: Screen compound libraries for active molecules against the target

items.label: Preclinical Testing
items.description: In vitro and animal model studies for safety and pharmacokinetics

items.label: Phase I — Safety
items.description: First-in-human trials with healthy volunteers to assess safety and dosing

items.label: Phase II — Efficacy
items.description: Controlled trials in patients to evaluate therapeutic efficacy

items.label: Phase III — Confirmation
items.description: Large multi-site trials to confirm benefit-risk profile

items.label: FDA Review & Approval
items.description: Regulatory submission, advisory committee review, and market authorization

data.caption: Iterative drug development from target discovery through regulatory approval
